title: plantago holosteum subsp. grovesii
intro: Plantago holosteum subsp. grovesii, also known as Groves' plantain, is a subspecies of the plantain plant that is native to Australia. It is a medicinal plant used for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The leaves of the plant have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling, ease congestion, and promote healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves can be made into a tea, applied topically as a poultice, or used in tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
